title: Profit Margin
shortDefinition: Net profit divided by revenue; shows what percentage of sales becomes actual profit.

content.definition: Profit margin (net margin) is the bottom line: (Revenue - All Expenses) / Revenue. If you generate 1M in revenue and 200K in net profit, your profit margin is 20%. It's the inverse of your burn rate—the percentage of each dollar sold that actually stays with the company. Gross margin is revenue minus COGS; operating margin is profit before interest and taxes; net margin is profit after everything. Each tells a different story about profitability.
content.whyItMatters: Profit margin is the ultimate scorecard of business efficiency. Two companies with identical revenue can have vastly different fates: one with 30% margins reaches profitability and scales sustainably; one with 5% margins is one downturn away from insolvency. Many founders chase top-line growth obsessed with vanity metrics while margins deteriorate. A 10% profit margin business generating 1B in revenue is objectively healthier than a 50% margin business generating 10M in revenue because scale matters. But scaling something unprofitable just accelerates losses—margins have to be viable.
content.howToApply: Track net margin monthly and set explicit targets by business stage: pre-revenue startups ignore it (you're in accumulation mode), growth-stage startups should be targeting 10-20%, mature SaaS companies 25%+. Understand the drivers of your margin: is it COGS dragging you down, or bloated OpEx? Break profit margin into components: gross margin x (1 - operating expense ratio). This decomposition shows where to focus. Benchmark yourself against competitors and industry standards—if you're 5 points below average, that's significant. Use profit margin to answer 'does this business model actually work?' If it doesn't at scale, fix the model before investing more money.
commonMistakes: Assuming profitability at scale when unit economics don't support it—margin math doesn't change with volume
Sacrificing margin for growth ('we'll make it up on volume')—this is how companies go bankrupt while scaling
Not distinguishing between gross and net margin, masking operational inefficiency
content.ideafuelConnection: IdeaFuel models profit margin under different scaling scenarios, shows margin sensitivity to key cost drivers, and helps identify which changes have the highest impact on profitability.

seoTitle: What is Profit Margin? | IdeaFuel Glossary
seoDescription: Understand profit margin and how to use it to measure and improve your startup's true profitability.
